category_title: Students who make disruptive noises to gain teacher attention during independent work
student_description: Tom disrupts independent work with high-pitched noises to gain teacher attention because he finds it easier than remembering to raise his hand. He uniquely requires strict differential attention (ignoring noises, attending to hand-raising), intermittent proximity, and proactive reminders of the functional benefits of hand-raising, rather than artificial rewards or constant hovering.

criteria.rationale: Reminding Tom of the plan before independent work begins reduces his temptation to escalate noises to steal attention.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan must include delivering reminders or class announcements about hand-raising expectations immediately prior to independent work time.

criteria.rationale: Tom needs to understand that hand-raising is not just a rule, but a more effective tool for getting the teacher attention he desires.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan must include explicitly teaching Tom the connection between raising his hand and successfully getting his needs (teacher attention) met.
